user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The rear of the vehicle is clearly visible, allowing for a complete assessment of the bumper and trunk area.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 shows severe damage to the rear of the vehicle, where the rear bumper cover is completely missing and the underlying bumper structure is heavily crushed and deformed.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. Photos upload kar diye hain.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images show two completely different vehicles, allowing for a clear determination of a claim mismatch.
claim_status_justification: The images show two entirely different vehicles: img_1 shows a white sedan with severe front-end crushing and a broken headlight, while img_2 shows a pristine white Jaguar with no damage. This mismatch contradicts the claim of a simple scratch on the front bumper.

user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. I added the pictures I have.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The two submitted images directly contradict each other regarding the windshield condition, making the evidence set unreliable for adjudication.
claim_status_justification: img_2 shows the windshield completely intact with no cracks or damage, directly conflicting with the shattered windshield visible in img_1 and with the user's claim of a spreading crack.

user_claim: Customer: Someone clipped my car while it was parked. | Support: What part was hit? | Customer: The side mirror got damaged. It is not sitting the way it should anymore. | Support: Was there any damage to the door too? | Customer: I did not notice anything else major, just the mirror.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The side mirror is clearly visible in the image, allowing for a direct inspection of its condition.
claim_status_justification: The image img_1 clearly shows that the glass of the side mirror is shattered, which supports the user's claim of damage to the side mirror.

user_claim: Customer: I want to file this as bumper damage. | Support: Can you tell me what happened? | Customer: The car was tapped from behind and now the back looks damaged. | Support: Are you asking for review of the rear side? | Customer: Yes, the back bumper. It looks pretty bad to me, so I uploaded both photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images show the rear of the vehicle clearly enough to evaluate the condition of the rear bumper.
claim_status_justification: While img_1 shows a close-up of a dented bumper panel, the full rear view of the vehicle in img_2 shows the rear bumper and bumper guard completely intact and free of any damage, contradicting the claim.

user_claim: Customer: Hi, I am not fully sure how to explain this because I noticed it only after reaching home. | Support: No problem, please tell me what happened. | Customer: There was a small bump earlier, nothing major, and I first thought everything was fine. Later I looked at the car again because my family member said the front area looked different. | Support: Are you reporting a general vehicle issue or a specific damaged part? | Customer: I was confused at first because I checked the side and the front and walked around the car a few times. I do not want to claim the wrong thing. | Support: That is okay. What part do you want reviewed? | Customer: After checking again, I think the issue is with the headlight. It looks like the headlight may be cracked, so please review that part.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The headlight is not visible in the provided image, which only shows the side profile of the vehicle.
claim_status_justification: The image img_1 shows the side of the car, including the door and side mirror, but the headlight is not visible. Therefore, there is not enough information to verify the claim.

user_claim: Customer: There is a dent along the side of my car. | Support: When did you notice it? | Customer: This morning, after the car had been parked near the gate. | Support: Is it near the front or back? | Customer: It is on the door panel area. I attached two photos from different distances.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 is heavily blurred and depicts a different vehicle than the black SUV in img_2, so the image set cannot reliably verify the claimed dent on the user's car door.
claim_status_justification: The two images show different vehicles and img_1 is too blurry to inspect; there is no usable image evidence confirming the claimed dent on the user's car door. (img_1, img_2)

user_claim: Customer: I picked up my car after service and noticed a mark on the hood. | Support: What kind of mark is it? | Customer: It looks like a scratch across the top panel. | Support: Do you think it happened during service? | Customer: Yes, that is why I am asking for review. The photo is attached.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the front of the vehicle, allowing for a full assessment of the hood and front panels.
claim_status_justification: The user claims a minor scratch on the hood after service, but the image shows a severely wrecked vehicle with major front-end damage and watermarked stock imagery (Vecteezy), completely contradicting the claim.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop fell from the table yesterday. | Support: Is it turning on? | Customer: It turns on, but the display glass has a crack now. | Support: Are you reporting the screen only or the whole laptop? | Customer: The screen is the issue. I attached a photo of it open.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The laptop screen is clearly visible in img_1 and shows severe glass shattering, sufficient to evaluate the claim.
claim_status_justification: img_1 clearly shows the laptop screen is severely shattered, directly supporting the user's claim of a cracked display glass.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop no longer opens smoothly. | Support: What seems to be wrong mechanically? | Customer: The hinge area has broken and the screen wobbles. | Support: Did this happen after a drop? | Customer: Yes, it slipped from the sofa. I uploaded photos from the outside and side.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted images show two different laptop models (a dark grey plastic laptop in img_1 and a silver aluminum laptop in img_2), creating a clear mismatch that prevents reliable verification of the claimed hinge damage.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows a dark grey laptop with apparent hinge damage, while img_2 shows a different, pristine silver laptop with no visible damage. The mismatch between the two images means the claim cannot be reliably verified against a consistent subject.

user_claim: Customer: I spilled water near my laptop while working. | Support: What part was affected? | Customer: It went over the keys and left a stain. Some keys feel sticky now. | Support: Is the display damaged too? | Customer: No, this is about the keyboard area only.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the laptop keyboard and surrounding body covered in water droplets, satisfying the visual evidence requirement for inspecting liquid damage.
claim_status_justification: The image img_1 clearly shows extensive water droplets spilled across the keyboard keys and the palm rest area, supporting the user's claim of water damage.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop bag fell from a chair and hit the floor. | Support: What damage did you notice? | Customer: One corner of the laptop has a dent now. | Support: Does the laptop still work? | Customer: It works, but the outer corner is damaged. I added two photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images provide clear views of the laptop's exterior and corners to evaluate the claim.
claim_status_justification: In img_1, the entire laptop lid and all four corners are clearly visible and completely free of any dents or damage. In img_2, the corner of the device is also completely smooth and undamaged, while a small dent is visible on the flat surface of the panel instead of the corner itself.

user_claim: Customer: I am not sure if this should be a repair claim or a replacement claim, so I wanted to ask first. | Support: We can help. What device is this about? | Customer: It is my laptop. I carry it every day, and yesterday my bag got pressed between two seats during travel. At first I thought the laptop was okay because it still opened. | Support: What changed after that? | Customer: Later, while using it, I noticed marks on the display area. I restarted it twice because I thought it might just be something on the screen, but it still bothered me. | Support: Are you reporting keyboard, hinge, body, or screen damage? | Customer: Not the keyboard or hinge. The issue I want checked is the screen. It looks shattered to me, so I am submitting this as screen damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the laptop screen with a prominent spiderweb crack pattern, satisfying the visual evidence requirements.
claim_status_justification: The image img_1 clearly shows a shattered laptop screen with a spiderweb crack pattern originating from the center of the display.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop trackpad has stopped working properly. | Support: Did anything happen before it stopped working? | Customer: The front area hit the desk edge when I moved it. | Support: Are you reporting internal function or physical damage? | Customer: Physical damage around the trackpad area. I attached the photo for review.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the trackpad area of the laptop with a minor scratch highlighted by a white circle.
claim_status_justification: The image img_1 shows a minor scratch on the lower edge of the trackpad, highlighted by a white circle, consistent with the user's claim of physical damage in that area after impact with a desk edge.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived damaged. | Support: What part of the package was affected? | Customer: One corner was crushed in when I received it. | Support: Is the item inside part of this claim? | Customer: Not right now. I am reporting the package corner damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the corner of the cardboard box, which is visibly crushed and deformed.
claim_status_justification: The image img_1 clearly shows that the corner of the cardboard box is crushed and dented, supporting the user's claim.

user_claim: Customer: Package receive hua toh opened jaisa lag raha tha. | Support: Tape broken tha ya box crush hua tha? | Customer: Seal wali side phati hui thi, jaise parcel khola gaya ho. | Support: Kya andar ka item missing hai? | Customer: Abhi item missing claim nahi kar raha, sirf torn packaging review karwana hai.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 clearly shows the package box and its seal/tape torn open on the side, which matches the user's claim of torn packaging at the seal.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows the brown adhesive tape sealing the box is torn open, directly supporting the user's claim of torn packaging at the seal. img_2 appears to be a generic stock photo and does not contribute independent evidence, so the non_original_image risk flag is retained.

user_claim: Customer: I received a package that looks water damaged. | Support: Was the item inside wet too? | Customer: I have not opened everything yet, but the outside has a wet-looking stain. | Support: So the claim is about the package condition? | Customer: Yes, the package surface looked damaged when it arrived.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the exterior of the cardboard box with a prominent wet stain.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 shows a cardboard box with a large, dark wet stain running down the side and corner, confirming the water damage claim.

user_claim: Customer: The item I ordered was not inside the box. | Support: Did the package look opened when you received it? | Customer: I checked it after delivery and could not find the product inside. | Support: What are you asking us to verify? | Customer: Please verify that the contents are missing from the package.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The interior of the box is filled with crumpled packing paper, which obstructs the view of the contents area and prevents verification of whether the item is missing or underneath.
claim_status_justification: In img_1, the box is filled with crumpled packing paper, which obstructs the view of the bottom of the box. It is not possible to determine if the item is missing or simply covered by the packing material.

user_claim: Customer: The shipping box arrived in bad condition. | Support: What kind of condition issue are you reporting? | Customer: It looked badly crushed when it was delivered. | Support: Is the claim about the outside box or the product inside? | Customer: The outside box. I want the crushed box reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image shows dented metal cans (items) instead of the claimed shipping box, providing direct evidence of a mismatch.
claim_status_justification: The user explicitly claimed the outside shipping box was crushed, but the image (img_1) shows dented metal cans (items) and does not show any shipping box, establishing a clear mismatch.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived opened. | Support: Was the package crushed or was the seal affected? | Customer: The seal area looked torn when I received it. | Support: Are you asking us to review the package condition or the item inside? | Customer: The package condition. I want the torn-open package reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted images are inconsistent and show two different boxes; img_2 is a watermarked stock photo and img_1 contains instruction-like text, so the image set is not usable for automated review.
claim_status_justification: The evidence is unreliable: img_1 shows a damaged box with a prompt-injection sticky note, and img_2 is an undamaged watermarked stock photo of a different box. The image set does not provide usable, consistent evidence to support or contradict the claim.
